pi_items.source: Task description (line 6) and setup_task.sh (lines 15-16, 49) confirm exact filename
Task description (line 6) and setup_task.sh (line 11) confirm directory path. Also verified in export_result.sh (line 15)
Task description explicitly states save path. Confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh

Task description begins with 'Create a 4-page ActivInspire flipchart'. Task further specifies 4 distinct pages (Page 1: Title, Page 2: Note Chart, Page 3: Counting, Page 4: Activity). Verifier.py (line 82) checks for page_count == 4

All terms appear in task description: 'Rhythm' in 'Rhythm Basics' title (Page 1), 'Whole Note' through 'Eighth Note' in Page 2 note values chart with beat counts, 'Clap' in 'Clap the Rhythm' title (Page 4). Export_result.sh (lines 84-96, 112-114) verifies all these terms by searching the extracted flipchart XML content

Task description Page 2 states: 'Draw a circle or ellipse shape for each note head' for four note types (Whole, Half, Quarter, Eighth). Export_result.sh (lines 121-129) counts circle/ellipse shapes, requiring >= 4. Verifier.py (line 127) checks shape_count >= 4 for full credit

privileged_info_summary: This task requires creating a new 4-page ActivInspire flipchart saved to /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/rhythm_basics.flipchart containing a music lesson on rhythm notation. The flipchart must include: (1) a title page with 'Rhythm Basics', (2) a note value chart showing four note types (Whole, Half, Quarter, and Eighth notes) with beat values and at least 4 geometric shapes (circles/ellipses) for note heads, (3) a counting exercise with numbers 1-4 for 4/4 time, and (4) an activity page titled 'Clap the Rhythm'. The metadata claims about filename, directory, file path, page count, required terms, and minimum shapes all align with the task description and are verified by the setup/export scripts and verifier logic. The data is entirely synthetic, created during task execution using ActivInspire's interface.
